Tests equality comparisons between NaN and RegExp, and NaN and Date.

# Purpose
This code performs two equality comparisons between `NaN` and two different JavaScript objects: `RegExp` and `Date`. The purpose is to evaluate whether `NaN` is equal to these objects, which will always result in `false` because `NaN` is not equal to any value, including itself, or any other object. This script demonstrates the behavior of the `==` operator when used with `NaN` and other JavaScript objects. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on illustrating the comparison behavior of `NaN`.
